title: Small forward

infobox.value: |  |  | | --- | --- | | 1 (Floor General) | * [Point guard](/wiki/Point_guard "Point guard") (PG) * [Combo guard](/wiki/Combo_guard "Combo guard") (PG/SG) | | 2 (Wing) | * [Shooting guard](/wiki/Shooting_guard "Shooting guard") (SG) * Guard-forward / [Swingman](/wiki/Swingman "Swingman") (GF , SG/SF or SG/PF) | | [An overview of a basketball court where players would be positions](/wiki/File:Basketball_positions.svg "Basketball half-court")

|  |  | | --- | --- | | 3 (Wing) | * Small forward (SF) * [Point forward](/wiki/Point_forward "Point forward") (PG/SF or PG/PF) | | 4 (Post) | * [Power forward](/wiki/Power_forward "Power forward") (PF) * Combo forward / [Stretch four](/wiki/Stretch_four "Stretch four") (SF/PF) |

(**SF**), also known as the **three**, is one of the [five positions](/wiki/Basketball_position "Basketball position") in a regulation [basketball](/wiki/Basketball "Basketball") game.[[2]](#cite_note-SGoodsSF-2) Small forwards are typically shorter, quicker, and leaner than [power forwards](/wiki/Power_forward_(basketball) "Power forward (basketball)") and [centers](/wiki/Center_(basketball) "Center (basketball)") but taller, larger, and stronger than either of the guard positions.[[2]](#cite_note-SGoodsSF-2) They are strategic and are often relied upon to score, defend, create open lanes, and rebound for their team.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

["Can Chelsea Gray guide Aces to back-to-back WNBA titles?"](https://www.espn.com/wnba/story/_/id/38624176/wnba-finals-2023-chelsea-gray-las-vegas-aces-point-gawd). *ESPN*. October 11, 2023. Retrieved June 9, 2024.

["The Ultimate Guide to Playing Small Forward"](https://web.archive.org/web/20150123002551/http://www.usab.com/youth/news/2011/06/the-ultimate-guide-to-playing-small-forward.aspx). *www.usab.com*. Archived from [the original](https://www.usab.com/youth/news/2011/06/the-ultimate-guide-to-playing-small-forward.aspx) on January 23, 2015. Retrieved 2022-11-01.
Recruiting Nation (November 28, 2006). ["Versatility is key for small forwards"](https://www.espn.com/college-sports/recruiting/news/story?id=2677310). *ESPN*.

Riches, Coach (2013-10-11). ["Basketball Player Positions Explained: Small Forward"](https://functionalbasketballcoaching.com/basketball-player-positions-explained-small-forward/). *Functional Basketball Coaching*. Retrieved 2022-11-01.
["Who are the top five small forwards?"](https://www.espn.com/wnba/story/_/id/9458895/wnba-wnba-top-five-small-forwards). ESPN. 8 July 2013. Retrieved 10 June 2024.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Basketball position

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/31/Lebron_2017.jpg/250px-Lebron_2017.jpg)](/wiki/File:Lebron_2017.jpg)

[LeBron James](/wiki/LeBron_James "LeBron James") (left) is now regarded as the greatest small forward in the history of the [NBA](/wiki/NBA "NBA").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/46/Maya_Moore_%2823%29_dribbles_around_Alex_Bentley_%2820%29_in_the_Minnesota_Lynx_vs_Atlanta_Dream_game.jpg/250px-Maya_Moore_%2823%29_dribbles_around_Alex_Bentley_%2820%29_in_the_Minnesota_Lynx_vs_Atlanta_Dream_game.jpg)](/wiki/File:Maya_Moore_(23)_dribbles_around_Alex_Bentley_(20)_in_the_Minnesota_Lynx_vs_Atlanta_Dream_game.jpg)

[Maya Moore](/wiki/Maya_Moore "Maya Moore") is regarded as one of the greatest small forwards in the history of the [WNBA](/wiki/Women%27s_National_Basketball_Association "Women's National Basketball Association").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

The **small forward** (**SF**), also known as the **three**, is one of the [five positions](/wiki/Basketball_position "Basketball position") in a regulation [basketball](/wiki/Basketball "Basketball") game.[[2]](#cite_note-SGoodsSF-2) Small forwards are typically shorter, quicker, and leaner than [power forwards](/wiki/Power_forward_(basketball) "Power forward (basketball)") and [centers](/wiki/Center_(basketball) "Center (basketball)") but taller, larger, and stronger than either of the guard positions.[[2]](#cite_note-SGoodsSF-2) They are strategic and are often relied upon to score, defend, create open lanes, and rebound for their team.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

The small forward is considered to be perhaps the most versatile of the five main basketball positions as they contribute offensively and defensively.[[4]](#cite_note-VersatilityIsKey-4) In the [NBA](/wiki/NBA "NBA"), small forwards typically range from 6' 6" (1.98 m) to 6' 9" (2.06 m); in the [WNBA](/wiki/WNBA "WNBA"), they are usually between 6' 0" (1.83 m) to 6' 2" (1.88 m). This puts them at the average height of all professional basketball players because they are taller than the [guards](/wiki/Guard_(basketball) "Guard (basketball)"), but shorter than the [power forward](/wiki/Power_forward_(basketball) "Power forward (basketball)") and [center.](/wiki/Center_(basketball) "Center (basketball)")[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Small forwards are responsible for scoring [points](/wiki/Point_(basketball) "Point (basketball)") and defending, and often are secondary or tertiary [rebounders](/wiki/Rebound_(basketball) "Rebound (basketball)") behind the power forward and center. In professional basketball, some have considerable passing responsibilities, and many are prolific scorers.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Style of play

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Small_forward&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Style of play")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/ed/Tayshaun_Prince.jpg/250px-Tayshaun_Prince.jpg)](/wiki/File:Tayshaun_Prince.jpg)

Former NBA small forward [Tayshaun Prince](/wiki/Tayshaun_Prince "Tayshaun Prince"), who became a crucial piece of the [Detroit Pistons](/wiki/Detroit_Pistons "Detroit Pistons") in the 2000s, was known for his ability to play both ends of the court.

The styles with which small forwards score vary widely. Some are very accurate shooters, such as [Kevin Durant](/wiki/Kevin_Durant "Kevin Durant"), and some prefer to initiate physical contact with opposing players, such as [LeBron James](/wiki/LeBron_James "LeBron James"). Other small forwards are often primarily [slashers](/wiki/Slasher_(basketball) "Slasher (basketball)") who also possess [jump shots](/wiki/Jump_shot_(basketball) "Jump shot (basketball)"). In some cases, small forwards position on the baseline; in other cases, they operate as off-ball specialists. The defensive specialists among them are notably versatile, often being able to guard multiple positions using their size, speed, and strength. Having adequate footwork, and a solid defensive stance contributes to why small forwards are successful.[[7]](#cite_note-7) When it comes to statistics, small forwards must be all over the map and at least have stats in most categories.

There are many small forwards that are well known for their contributions and versatility. Top small forwards in the history of the WNBA include: [Maya Moore](/wiki/Maya_Moore "Maya Moore"), [Angel McCoughtry](/wiki/Angel_McCoughtry "Angel McCoughtry"), [Tamika Catchings](/wiki/Tamika_Catchings "Tamika Catchings"), [Elena Delle Donne](/wiki/Elena_Delle_Donne "Elena Delle Donne"), and [DeWanna Bonner](/wiki/DeWanna_Bonner "DeWanna Bonner").[[8]](#cite_note-8) Small forwards inducted in the [Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame](/wiki/Naismith_Memorial_Basketball_Hall_of_Fame "Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Fame") include [Julius Erving](/wiki/Julius_Erving "Julius Erving"), [Larry Bird](/wiki/Larry_Bird "Larry Bird"), [James Worthy](/wiki/James_Worthy "James Worthy"), [Elgin Baylor](/wiki/Elgin_Baylor "Elgin Baylor"), [Paul Pierce](/wiki/Paul_Pierce "Paul Pierce"), [Vince Carter](/wiki/Vince_Carter "Vince Carter"), [John Havlicek](/wiki/John_Havlicek "John Havlicek"), [Scottie Pippen](/wiki/Scottie_Pippen "Scottie Pippen"), [Alex English](/wiki/Alex_English "Alex English"), [Bernard King](/wiki/Bernard_King "Bernard King"), [Chris Mullin](/wiki/Chris_Mullin "Chris Mullin"), [Dominique Wilkins](/wiki/Dominique_Wilkins "Dominique Wilkins"), [Rick Barry](/wiki/Rick_Barry "Rick Barry"), [Grant Hill](/wiki/Grant_Hill "Grant Hill"), [Tamika Catchings](/wiki/Tamika_Catchings "Tamika Catchings"), [Cheryl Miller](/wiki/Cheryl_Miller "Cheryl Miller"), and [Sheryl Swoopes](/wiki/Sheryl_Swoopes "Sheryl Swoopes").

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Small_forward&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: See also")]

* [Cheryl Miller Award](/wiki/Cheryl_Miller_Award "Cheryl Miller Award")
